clean_body: Voting Records Matter | | | | | | | | | | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE March 16, 2023 Contact: Chris Farmer 804 912 6007 | Senator Chase Wins Tuesday Night's Virginia Citizens Defense League Debate | 2A VOTING RECORDS MATTER | Colonial Heights, VA \- Senator Amanda Chase was the clear winner at the VDCL Senate District 12 Debate that took place Thursday evening at the Colonial Heights American Legion Post 284. "Senator Chase was the only candidate on the stage with a solid 8 year 100% uncompromising VCDL voting record that we can trust. Her knowledge of the questions was spot on. She nailed the answers. It is clear her opponents are both moderates and have no command or knowledge of most 2A questions that were asked. Senator Chase was also the only candidate that was carrying on stage. She has my vote," owner of TAC Solutions Dee Dee Van Buren. "Senator Chase was the only candidate who answered the questions and was on point with her answers. She clearly showed her knowledge and expertise with every question that was asked, while her opponents could only answer questions generally," said owner of TAC Solutions Dave Van Buren. Her opponent former State Senator Glen Sturtevant apologized for his bad 2A votes during his one term in the Senate to include voting for red flag laws. He apologized profusely and asked 2A supporters to give him a second chance. During the debate, Sturtevant replied that he would give localities the ability to decide which schools could allow armed law enforcement. Senator Chase said she disagreed with Sturtevant and that she would allow any law abiding citizen to conceal carry under any circumstance and that the Constitution, not a locality should determine where individuals were allowed to carry for personal protection. Her second opponent Tina Ramirez, who has no voting record on gun rights and has not been involved with the NRA or VCDL nor has she attended any of their rallies or gun shows, could only speculate that she would support gun rights. She also mentioned she was leaving for Iraq tomorrow. Some attendees asked how would she serve in the Senate if she is constantly out of the country for months. Senator Chase was the clear winner of this debate. | "Voting records matter. Senator Amanda Chase is the only candidate and incumbent senator running in Senate District 12, which includes Chesterfield and Colonial Heights to receive a perfect lifetime score of 100% for all 8 years she's served in the Virginia Senate. Senator Chase has a proven track record of not only defending the 2nd Amendment, but actively working to repeal harmful gun control legislation passed between 2020 and 2022. We strongly encourage the 2A community to join us in supporting Senator Amanda Chase," Dave and Dee Dee VanBuren. | Senator Chase responded, "I am humbled to receive the endorsement of Dave and DeeDee VanBuren, owners of TAC Solutions who train thousands of 2A supporters, not only in Senate District 12 but across Virginia. | In case you missed the VCDL Virginia Citizens Defense League Senate District 12 Debate last night, Senator Chase won the debate hands down. Senator Amanda Chase, voting record, lifetime 8 year 100% VCDL rating Glen Sturtevant, voting record, 96%, 93%, 80%, 88% ratings Tina Ramirez, no voting record | Senator Amanda Chase 8 Year "Perfect" Pro-2A VOTING RECORD | | | | | | | | | | Most Proven 2A Choice for Senate District 12 | Re-elect Senator Amanda Chase to SD12 (Chesterfield and Colonial Heights) | | | ---|--- | Lifetime "A" Rating with the NRA Lifetime 100% Rating with VCDL Always carries for self-defense Always votes NO on Red Flag laws Always votes for Constitutional Carry Sponsored bills to restore 2A rights | www.chaseforsenate.com | Former one-term Senator Glen Sturtevant wants to challenge Virginia State Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate in District 12. Voting records matter. Any candidate can SAY they will support 2A. But there's ONLY ONE that has done it. We can trust Senator Amanda Chase. 100 % lifetime rating with VCDL (Virginia Citizen's Defense League) That's an 8 year voting record you can trust. With every year that Glen Sturtevant was in office his voting record got worse. | | | | | | | | 2016 2A RECORD Amanda Chase 100 Glen Sturtevant 96 | | | 2017 2A RECORD Amanda Chase 100 Glen Sturtevant 93 | | | 2018 2A RECORD Amanda Chase 100 Glen Sturtevant 80 | | | 2019 2A RECORD Amanda Chase 100 Glen Sturtevant 88 | VOTING RECORDS MATTER | The longer he was in office, the worse his 2A votes became. See for yourself. Why would any Republican support Glen Sturtevant again? | | | | | ---|--- | FORMER Senator Glen Sturtevant's 2nd Amendment Voting Record ... 2016 96% 2017 93% 2018 80% 2019 88% 2019 FAILED TO BE RE-ELECTED "Are you willing to take a chance that Glen will protect your gun rights?" == | Watch Senator Chase as she hits every target | Or trust Senator Amanda Chase? 2016 100% 2017 100% 2018 100% 2019 100% 2020 100% 2021 100% 2022 100% The choice is OBVIOUS if you value the 2nd Amendment. You want a fighter not a compromiser to stand up for your 2A rights. Re-elect Senator Amanda Chase | Since 2016, Senator Chase has also introduced 18 bills in support of law abiding gun owners and to reverse the Gun Grab of 2020 | Senator Chase passed 2 pro-2A Bills even under a Democrat controlled Senate & Governor | 2023 2A Legislation introduced by Senator Chase: SB806 Repeal gun free zones by localities Control of firearms by localities. Removes the authority for a locality by ordinance to prohibit the possession or carrying of firearms, ammunition, or components or any combination thereof in (i) any building, or part thereof, owned or used by such locality for governmental purposes; (ii) any public park owned or operated by the locality; (iii) any recreation or community center facility; or (iv) any public street, road, alley, or sidewalk or public right-of-way or any other place of whatever nature that is open to the public and is being used by or is adjacent to a permitted event or an event that would otherwise require a permit. The bill provides that any firearm received by the locality pursuant to a buy-back program shall be offered for sale by public auction or sealed bids to a person licensed as a dealer. Current law provides that any such firearm shall be destroyed by the locality unless the person surrendering the firearm requests in writing that the firearm be offered for sale. The bill also limits the authority of localities to bring lawsuits against certain firearms manufacturers and others and further provides that the right to bring any such action is reserved exclusively to the Commonwealth and shall be brought by the Attorney General. == SB1488 Repeal of red flag laws Firearms; removal from persons posing substantial risk; penalties. Repeals the procedure by which any attorney for the Commonwealth or law-enforcement officer may apply to a general district court, circuit court, or juvenile and domestic relations district court judge or magistrate for an emergency substantial risk order to prohibit a person who poses a substantial risk of injury to himself or others from purchasing, possessing, or transporting a firearm. The bill also removes the substantial risk order registry for the entry of orders issued. == 2022 2A Legislation introduced by Senator Chase: SB74 Repeal of Gun Free Zones by Localities Control of firearms by localities. Removes the authority for a locality by ordinance to prohibit the possession or carrying of firearms, ammunition, or components or any combination thereof in (i) any building, or part thereof, owned or used by such locality for governmental purposes; (ii) any public park owned or operated by the locality; (iii) any recreation or community center facility; or (iv) any public street, road, alley, or sidewalk or public right-of-way or any other place of whatever nature that is open to the public and is being used by or is adjacent to a permitted event or an event that would otherwise require a permit. The bill provides that any firearm received by the locality pursuant to a buy-back program shall be offered for sale by public auction or sealed bids to a person licensed as a dealer. Current law provides that any such firearm shall be destroyed by the locality unless the person surrendering the firearm requests in writing that the firearm be offered for sale. The bill also limits the authority of localities and state governmental entities to bring lawsuits against certain firearms manufacturers and others. == SB75 Repeal of Gun Free Zones at the State Capitol and State Buildings Carrying a firearm or explosive material within Capitol Square and the surrounding area, into building owned or leased by the Commonwealth, etc.; penalty. Repeals the prohibition on carrying a firearm or explosive material within (i) the Capitol of Virginia; (ii) Capitol Square and the surrounding area; (iii) any building owned or leased by the Commonwealth or any agency thereof; or (iv) any office where employees of the Commonwealth or any agency thereof are regularly present for the purpose of performing their official duties. Current law makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person to carry a firearm or explosive material within these areas, subject to certain exceptions, including the following individuals while acting in the conduct of such person's official duties: any law-enforcement officer, any authorized security personnel, any active military personnel, any fire marshal when such fire marshal has been granted police powers, or any member of a cadet corps while such member is participating in an official ceremonial event for the Commonwealth. == 2021 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB1238 Allows US Armed Forces Veterans to Serve as School Security Officers School security officers; carrying of firearms; United States Armed Forces veterans; employees of local school boards. Permits local school boards to authorize the carrying of a firearm by an individual hired as a school security officer who is (i) an honorably discharged veteran of the United States Armed Forces or (ii) an employee of a local school board who has completed an approved auxiliary training academy. == 2020 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB825 Repeals Prohibition to Carry loaded firearms in public areas prohibited and in government buildings Carrying loaded firearms. Prohibits carrying loaded firearms in government buildings. == SB901 Allows residents to carry concealed handguns without acquiring a concealed carry permit. Concealed handgun permits. Allows residents to carry concealed handguns without acquiring a concealed carry permit. == SB958 Repeals the prohibition and allows a law abiding citizen to carry for protection while at church Carrying dangerous weapon to place of religious worship. Repeals the statutory prohibition on carrying a gun, pistol, bowie knife, dagger, or other dangerous weapon, without good and sufficient reason, to a place of worship while a meeting for religious purposes is being held at such place. == SB1009 If a state or locality creates a gun free zone they lose their sovereign immunity. Firearm-free zones designated by the Commonwealth or a locality; regulation of weapons; waiver of sovereign immunity. Provides that (i) if the Commonwealth designates any property owned by it as a firearm-free zone or (ii) if any locality designates such locality or any part of such locality as a firearm-free zone, the Commonwealth or such locality waives its sovereign immunity as it relates to any injuries sustained by persons lawfully present in such firearm-free zone. The bill further provides that if the Commonwealth or a locality adopts any ordinance, rule, policy, or regulation regulating weapons, the Commonwealth or locality assumes an affirmative duty to protect invitees lawfully on the premises of the Commonwealth or locality and establishes a waiver of sovereign immunity for any governmental entity or official responsible for such regulation. 2019 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB1012 Allows Firefighters and EMS to Conceal Carry when they're on duty Carrying a concealed handgun; firefighters and emergency medical services providers. Provides that any firefighter or person employed as emergency medical services personnel may carry a concealed handgun while engaged in the performance of his official duties, provided that such firefighter or person employed as emergency medical services personnel has been approved to carry a concealed handgun by his fire chief or emergency medical services chief. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services, in consultation with the Office of Emergency Medical Services of the Department of Health and the Department of Fire Programs, to develop a model policy regarding carrying a concealed handgun for firefighters and emergency medical services personnel. == 2018 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB715 Allows Firefighters and EMS to Conceal Carry even when they're on duty Carrying a concealed handgun; firefighters and emergency medical services providers. Provides that any firefighter or person employed as emergency medical services personnel may carry a concealed handgun while engaged in the performance of his official duties, provided that such firefighter or person employed as emergency medical services personnel has been approved to carry a concealed handgun by his fire chief or emergency medical services chief. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services, in consultation with the Office of Emergency Medical Services of the Department of Health and the Department of Fire Programs, to develop a model policy regarding carrying a concealed handgun for firefighters and emergency medical services personnel. == SB912 Retired law-enforcement officers; carrying a concealed handgun; return to work Retired law-enforcement officers; carrying a concealed handgun; return to work. Clarifies that a retired law-enforcement officer shall surrender his proof of consultation to carry a concealed handgun when he returns to work as a law-enforcement officer. Current law does not specify that his return to work be as a law-enforcement officer. == PASSED & SIGNED INTO LAW 2017 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB791 Reduces fee for getting a conceal handgun permit Fees for concealed handgun permits. Makes the $10 fee that the clerk of court is now required to charge for processing a concealed handgun permit application or issuing a concealed handgun permit discretionary with the clerk. == SB1422 Reduces fee for getting a conceal handgun permit Local law enforcement; fees for concealed handgun permits; courthouse and courtroom security; assessment. Eliminates (i) the fee, under current law up to $35, that a local law-enforcement agency is permitted to charge for conducting the background investigation for a concealed handgun permit and (ii) the requirement that the local law-enforcement agency forward to the State Police any amount assessed by the FBI for providing criminal history record information in the background investigation. The bill makes discretionary the current mandatory fee of up to $10 charged by the clerk for processing a concealed handgun permit application or issuing a permit. The bill increases from $10 to $20 the maximum amount, designated solely to fund courthouse and courtroom security, that a local governing body may assess against a convicted defendant as part of the costs in a criminal or traffic case in district or circuit court. == SB1450 Conceal Carry Holders can carry on colleges and university campuses Possession of concealed handguns; concealed handgun permit holders at public institutions of higher education. Allows any firefighter, person employed as search and rescue personnel, person employed as emergency medical services personnel, certified campus security officer, or student, employee, contractor, vendor, or faculty member of a public institution of higher education that participates in a Reserve Officers' Training Corps (ROTC) program who possesses a valid Virginia concealed handgun permit or a concealed handgun permit from another state that is recognized by Virginia to carry a concealed handgun on the property of, in buildings owned by, or at events hosted at public institutions of higher education. == 2016 2A Legislation Introduced by Senator Chase: SB615 Allows retired law enforcement to purchase their firearm at fair market value; PASSED Purchase of weapons other than handguns by certain officers. Allows officers of certain agencies to purchase, at a fair market price and with approval of the agency head, weapons other than handguns that are issued for personal use of an officer so long as the weapon is a type and configuration that can be purchased at a regular hardware or sporting goods store by a private citizen without restrictions other than the instant background check. Current law allows this type of purchase only for the Department of State Police. This bill is identical to == == SB616 Reciprocity with other states Out-of-state concealed handgun permits; photo identification. Removes certain requirements for an out-of-state concealed handgun permit or license to be recognized and accepted in Virginia and instead requires an out-of-state permit or license holder to carry a government-issued photo identification and present his permit or license and such identification upon demand by a law-enforcement officer in order for his out-of-state permit or license to be recognized and accepted in Virginia. This bill was incorporated into == == PASSED & SIGNED INTO LAW SB677 Reduce fees to get a conceal carry permit Fees for concealed handgun permits. Makes the $10 fee that the clerk of court is now required to charge for processing a concealed handgun permit application or issuing a concealed handgun permit discretionary with the clerk. == | Senator Chase is the only TRUSTED 2A Candidate in Senate District 12 | Senator Chase spoke at the largest VCDL in Virginia's history | | | | | | | | | | | | | Senator Chase has also been endorsed by Dick Heller | | Senator Chase has also been Endorsed by Dick Heller, D.C. vs. Heller, Supreme Court Gun Freedom Case | "I know of no harder working person in Virginia so capable and willing to fight for freedom and our foundational God-given natural rights through self-preservation through our United States Constitutional Second Amendment than Senator Amanda Chase," Dick Heller. == == | For God and country, | | | | | | | | == | | == | | == | | | | | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. body:
Ohio Democrats have voted to endorse the reproductive rights ballot initiative — which means it's crucial that we reach the $25,000 fundraising goal we've set to help collect the signatures needed to get reproductive rights on the ballot.
| Did you read Liz's last email, team?

While we've seen an influx of donations from folks since Liz's last email, **we're still falling short.**

**[With the future of reproductive rights on the line, we can't afford to miss a single goal we set! Will you chip in $5, or whatever you can, to help us reach our goal and kick off our signature-gathering efforts?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60297927/396653544/-205309248?refcode=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=4398764b-47c4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Mzk4NzY0Yi00N2M0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OSW_SmQXyvekqnvAWwVrv3bWXolGNIrUbot0_N9zQgE=)**

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Liz Walters, Team ODP
Date: Mar 16, 2023
Subject: We need to hit the ground running on our signature-gathering efforts
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60297928/396653545/-205309248?refcode=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=4398764b-47c4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Mzk4NzY0Yi00N2M0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OSW_SmQXyvekqnvAWwVrv3bWXolGNIrUbot0_N9zQgE=) Team, I want to be clear about something:

**Ohio is a pro-choice state. And the Ohio Democratic Party is proud to have endorsed a reproductive rights ballot initiative that would enshrine the right to bodily autonomy in our state constitution.**

Gathering signatures to qualify **[takes resources, so I'm counting on everyone on this people-powered team to help us close the gap in our ambitious $25,000 fundraising goal. Will you chip in $5 before our mid-month deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60297929/396653546/-205309248?refcode=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=4398764b-47c4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Mzk4NzY0Yi00N2M0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OSW_SmQXyvekqnvAWwVrv3bWXolGNIrUbot0_N9zQgE=)**

name: OcasioCortez.com
subject: Marijuana reform
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
A good news alert.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230316-4012-header&t=1&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)
It’s not every day we have good news to share from Congress. But even in the minority, Alexandria is moving the needle and finding opportunities for bipartisan support.
**Even with President Biden’s federal action last year, minor cannabis offenses continue to hold back so many Americans — disproportionately people of color — from employment, housing, and other opportunities.** The vast majority of these criminal charges exist at the state and local government, where expungements can be impeded by limited resources and outdated technology.
But alongside Republican David Joyce (OH-14), Alexandria has introduced the HOPE Act (_H_ arnessing _O_ pportunities by _P_ ursuing _E_ xpungement), designed to grant federal resources to digitize records, create legal relief clinics, and expedite the expungement process while supporting the individuals going through it.
[Not only could this bill be life-changing for the hundreds of thousands of people whose low-level charges prevent them from reaching their full potential, it’s proof that Alexandria’s fight for dignity for all transcends party lines. If you can afford a $3 donation today, we ask you to support her fight today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230316.6YhXuH&refcode=em20230316-4012-P_FND_EGR_LEG&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)
The HOPE Act nearly garnered enough support to move forward in Alexandria’s last term. Democrats rallied behind two marijuana reform bills — the HOPE Act and the SAFE Banking Act — and were able to open conversations with Republican lawmakers in the lame duck period as the last Congress ended.
[Alexandria’s work to open opportunities for low-level offenders is bigger than partisan politics, and she is only able to do it with your grassroots support. If you can, your $3 donation today goes a long way in making new opportunities available to all.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230316.6YhXuH&refcode=em20230316-4012-P_FND_EGR_LEG&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)
In solidarity,
Team AOC
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230316.6YhXuH&refcode=em20230316-4012-P_FND_EGR_LEG&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)**

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230316?refcode=em20230316-4012-P_FND_EGR_LEG&t=23&refcode2=4012_2526796_Cuw1qB&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230316-4012-P_FND_EGR_LEG&t=24&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=25&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=26&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=27&akid=4012%2E2526796%2ECuw1qB)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

Even with President Biden’s federal action last year, minor cannabis offenses continue to hold back so many Americans — disproportionately people of color — from employment, housing, and other opportunities. The vast majority of these criminal charges exist at the state and local government, where expungements can be impeded by limited resources and outdated technology. But alongside Republican David Joyce (OH-14), Alexandria has introduced the HOPE Act (H arnessing O pportunities by P ursuing E xpungement), designed to grant federal resources to digitize records, create legal relief clinics, and expedite the expungement process while supporting the individuals going through it. name: Jennifer Wexton
subject: What we do now will allow us to reach voters all across my district
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wexton for Congress
body:
Only a few hours remain until our mid-month fundraising deadline.

[**Can you donate $19 (our current average email gift) or more right now? We need to raise another $1,201 by midnight, but we won't get there without grassroots supporters like you.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60298520/396655354/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230316MM2_MM_JW_FULL_5_NG_N_L1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&amount=19&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmU2YWJjNi00YWM0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=548E-6JkqCt14oaJcEJtc0Hb-UxOt6rwKfhqVcJQyjY=)

Republicans improved by 11 percent in my district last cycle, which means that VA-10 is high on their target list going into 2024. So we're leaving nothing to chance and are gearing up now for a tough fight in the months ahead.

I'm excited to share my record with all the voters across VA-10, but we'll need the resources to overcome sustained and misleading messaging from the GOP. The money we raise from emails like this is essential for our field outreach, our digital outreach, and our eventual TV ads.

**So please, chip in to help us meet our goal and ensure that our message ends up reaching every corner of the district:** [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jw_em**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60298521/396655355/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230316MM2_MM_JW_FULL_5_NG_N_L2&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmU2YWJjNi00YWM0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=548E-6JkqCt14oaJcEJtc0Hb-UxOt6rwKfhqVcJQyjY=)

Thank you!

name: Ratings Alert (via Democrats.org)
subject: Getting into the weeds on 2024 race ratings
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, **[www
body:
Recent Cook Political Report ratings show that the race for control of the House, Senate, and governorships across the country are going to be incredibly competitive. | |
---
The first Cook Political Report ratings for 2024 just dropped -- and they show that the race for control of the House, Senate, and governorships across the country will be _incredibly_ competitive. Here's the long and short of it: 🚨We have a number of highly competitive races across the country, **with several key seats rated as toss-ups** ; 🚨Republicans are going to pour resources into the most competitive districts; 🚨We’ll need to be strategic about where we allocate resources. We can defend our Senate majority, retake the House, and win governorships across the country, but we need your support. When you donate to the DNC, you’re investing in the electoral infrastructure Democrats are relying on to win races up and down the ballot -- will you pitch in $7 today, Derek?

# A bunch of **[RINO's took money from Bill Gates](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-k/)** during the 2022 election season, is your representative on the list?
**Fake Letter to the President** Biden [**tweeted a handwritten letter he allegedly received from a little girl asking him to "fix" the gender wage gap**](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-u/). The internet scorched him for it, with many suggesting it was fake. _Do you think this is real?_ **Woke Bank**
## Failed **[Silicon Valley Bank donated over $73 MILLION to Black Lives Matter](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-o/)** -related social justice groups before it collapsed.
**Just Say No** Tucker Carlson says **[if we don't start making lots of noise about the banking crisis](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-x/)** , we will see a government-controlled digital currency. **End Zuckerbucks** A Republican congresswoman's **[new bill would ban nonprofits from donating to election organizations](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-m/) - **after accusations Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg used big bucks to pedal political influence in 2020**.**

**Let****'s[ have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-c/)!**

* She **[promised](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-a/).**
* Ukraine National**[Bank](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-f/).**
* The dog has**[demands](https://caucusroom.cmail19.com/t/i-l-qkitjjk-trhiujldw-z/).**

Team, **March 31 is our campaign’s first FEC fundraising deadline of 2023.** Here’s why that matters:
1. When the financial quarter ends, we have to provide our fundraising numbers to the FEC, which will go public for everyone — including the far-right — to see.

2. Gabe was just named a DCCC Frontline member and a top target of the GOP, since he won this toss-up seat by just 1,350 votes last November. If we’re going to flip the House blue next year, we NEED to defend this seat!

3. Fundraising has been slow since the last election ended, and if we’re going to go up against the far-right and their dark money, we need to build as much grassroots resources as possible.

clean_body: | = Team, March 31 is our campaign’s first FEC fundraising deadline of 2023. Here’s why that matters: 1. When the financial quarter ends, we have to provide our fundraising numbers to the FEC, which will go public for everyone — including the far-right — to see. 2. Gabe was just named a DCCC Frontline member and a top target of the GOP, since he won this toss-up seat by just 1,350 votes last November. If we’re going to flip the House blue next year, we NEED to defend this seat! 3. I’m so honored to have received this recognition of my work as a pro-choice advocate. Being the first pro-choice woman to represent this district in Congress is a tremendous responsibility and one that I do not take lightly.

With reproductive rights constantly under attack in our country – including a potential nationwide ban on an abortion pill coming as soon as today – **we cannot slow down for even a moment in our fight to make reproductive autonomy the law of the land.**

What a lot of people don’t know about EMILY’s List is that EMILY is an acronym. It stands for _Early Money Is Like Yeast_ – because it raises the dough! (Get it?)

I couldn’t agree with this sentiment more – **that’s why I’m asking you to make an early investment in my re-election campaign today.** Every early dollar raised at this point in the election cycle makes an outsized difference down the line. clean_body: I’m so honored to have received this recognition of my work as a pro-choice advocate. | = | Big news, folks: my re-election campaign has officially been endorsed by EMILY’s List, the top organization in the country dedicated to electing pro-choice Democratic women up and down the ballot. = | = I’m so honored to have received this recognition of my work as a pro-choice advocate. Being the first pro-choice woman to represent this district in Congress is a tremendous responsibility and one that I do not take lightly. With reproductive rights constantly under attack in our country – including a potential nationwide ban on an abortion pill coming as soon as today – we cannot slow down for even a moment in our fight to make reproductive autonomy the law of the land. What a lot of people don’t know about EMILY’s List is that EMILY is an acronym. It stands for Early Money Is Like Yeast – because it raises the dough! (Get it?) I couldn’t agree with this sentiment more – that’s why I’m asking you to make an early investment in my re-election campaign today. Every early dollar raised at this point in the election cycle makes an outsized difference down the line. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Gratefully, Hillary * | | = Paid for by Scholten for Congress Hillary Scholten for Congress PO Box 6233 Grand Rapids, MI 49510 United States | = If you would like to receive fewer emails, . name: Katherine Clark
subject: Building the future that our daughters deserve
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Susan Wild for Congress
body:
Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60299716/396659439/1192878862?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230316-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDljN2I1NS01MGM0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fgPU4uHJC2-L5BfEx4OEx49Bkb5h8Xx1O1ZnBbjVAZA=)
---
Happy Women’s History Month, Peter!

My name is Katherine Clark, and I proudly serve Massachusetts’ 5th district in Congress. I am also in House leadership serving as the Democratic Whip.

When I first came to Congress in 2013, there weren’t many women serving alongside me. Multiple times, I was stopped and mistaken for someone’s spouse, and I had a hard time even getting into the Chamber.

Thankfully, walking through the halls of Congress in 2023 is much different. **There are more women serving in Congress than ever before — 93 women in the House Democratic Caucus alone!**

I’ve been adamant about helping elect more women to Congress, because the truth is that for generations, we’ve failed to address the persistent challenges faced by women, especially by women of color.

**And with MAGA extremists in the House majority threatening to take us back even further, the stakes of our work couldn’t be higher.

[_That’s why I’m writing to you today, Peter. We need strong women in Congress, and I’m asking you to support that effort by splitting a contribution between my campaign and Susan Wild to elect more women into office._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60299717/396659440/1192878862?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230316-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDljN2I1NS01MGM0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fgPU4uHJC2-L5BfEx4OEx49Bkb5h8Xx1O1ZnBbjVAZA=)**

Effective leadership isn’t about individual ambition. It’s about our collective good.

Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better.

**Together, Susan Wild and I will continue the fight for gender equity and women’s rights, and with your support, elect even more candidates who are committed to building the kind of country and future that our daughters deserve.

[_So please, if you can afford to, split a contribution today between my campaign and Susan Wild and help us fulfill the country’s promise of equality and opportunity for all._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60299723/396659446/1192878862?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230316-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDljN2I1NS01MGM0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fgPU4uHJC2-L5BfEx4OEx49Bkb5h8Xx1O1ZnBbjVAZA=)**

Thank you for your support, Peter! I hope you take some time this month to reflect on our achievements, celebrate the women in your life, and recommit to building a stronger, more inclusive America.

All my best,

— Katherine

Representative Katherine Clark
Massachusetts 5th Congressional District
Democratic Whip

clean_body: Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better. | = Happy Women’s History Month, Peter! My name is Katherine Clark, and I proudly serve Massachusetts’ 5th district in Congress. I am also in House leadership serving as the Democratic Whip. When I first came to Congress in 2013, there weren’t many women serving alongside me. Multiple times, I was stopped and mistaken for someone’s spouse, and I had a hard time even getting into the Chamber. Thankfully, walking through the halls of Congress in 2023 is much different. There are more women serving in Congress than ever before — 93 women in the House Democratic Caucus alone! I’ve been adamant about helping elect more women to Congress, because the truth is that for generations, we’ve failed to address the persistent challenges faced by women, especially by women of color. And with MAGA extremists in the House majority threatening to take us back even further, the stakes of our work couldn’t be higher. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Effective leadership isn’t about individual ambition. It’s about our collective good. Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better. Together, Susan Wild and I will continue the fight for gender equity and women’s rights, and with your support, elect even more candidates who are committed to building the kind of country and future that our daughters deserve. = Thank you for your support, Peter! body:
| | | **ALERT: Experts warn that Social Security could be on the Supreme Court's chopping block**

_We urgently need to know where you stand on this issue -- but we have**NO RECORD** of your answer:_

[](http://go.dscc.org/go/12268?ask=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&akid=10163%2E9716520%2EmHmoNB)
**Should the Supreme Court UPHOLD the constitutionality of Social Security?**

| | | [Yes](http://go.dscc.org/go/12268?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&akid=10163%2E9716520%2EmHmoNB)

---

Team -- By overturning _Roe v. Wade_ , the Supreme Court has proven that **_NOTHING_** is safe from the conservative justices' right-wing majority.

**In fact, experts warn that Social Security could be in danger, too.** During her confirmation hearings, Amy Coney Barrett famously REFUSED to say if Social Security was constitutional -- and if the Court strikes it down, **_65 million Americans_ will LOSE the benefits they earned.**

We can’t risk losing this _**bedrock**_ of Americans' retirement, so we’ve launched an urgent Live Poll to PROVE that the American people support upholding Social Security -- _but we’re still short 29 responses in Pennsylvania! _

**Please, answer before midnight:****Should the Supreme Court UPHOLD the constitutionality of Social Security?**

| | [Yes](http://go.dscc.org/go/12268?ask=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230316-SupremeCourtUpdate-SCOTUSSocialSecurityRF-Pros-sl1&akid=10163%2E9716520%2EmHmoNB)

I know it is late, Peter, but I needed to reach out one more time before our critical midnight deadline.
Democrats have been delivering for the American people. From lowering prescription drug costs and confirming a diverse group of highly qualified judges, to tackling climate change and rebuilding our transportation infrastructure.
**But our historic progress is in jeopardy.** If we don’t continue to grow our resources, the GOP will have a stronger advantage in 2024.
**[Here is my ask: Our mid-month deadline is tonight and we are running short. Can you chip in $5 right now if you are committed to keeping the Democratic majority in the Senate?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60299908/396660266/-94572091?refcode=dd_em_230316_1_t1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDdmNGU3My01M2M0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KkPD-jXQIy4zUZuEZ652irf8619OBtNkBpxZtRabqzU=)**

disclaimer_text: Paid for by the HER Time PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60300104/396660521/-2095412496?refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230316_PM2_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTViM2I4MjUtNTRjNC1lZDExLWE4ZTAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hBi-uUtk5b_nE9kvbLtOl2Kf6BrbeS7ff-FDdhj0gGE=)
---
Peter – Yesterday’s hearing on the legality of the abortion pill in the United States was a stark reminder of just how fragile our rights are in this country. Access to this most common form of abortion care can be stripped away by one unelected judge on any given day. And if it’s not by a right-wing judge, major pharmacies can control our access — several have already vowed not to provide the pill, even in states where abortion remains legal. We should not have to fight so hard for our basic human rights — but since that’s the reality we live in in a post-_Roe_ world, we’ve created the Abortion Pill Hearing Rapid Response Fund. This is an emergency fund that will help us respond to whatever decision Judge Kacsmaryk hands down. [_**We don’t know when it’s coming, but when it does, we’ll be ready. clean_body: | = Peter – Yesterday’s hearing on the legality of the abortion pill in the United States was a stark reminder of just how fragile our rights are in this country. Access to this most common form of abortion care can be stripped away by one unelected judge on any given day. And if it’s not by a right-wing judge, major pharmacies can control our access — several have already vowed not to provide the pill, even in states where abortion remains legal. We should not have to fight so hard for our basic human rights — but since that’s the reality we live in in a post-Roe world, we’ve created the Abortion Pill Hearing Rapid Response Fund. This is an emergency fund that will help us respond to whatever decision Judge Kacsmaryk hands down. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks!
